mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and remains elevated, the real discount rate applied to long-duration assets increases, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as investors demand compensation for opportunity cost. This is an always-on trend mechanism reflecting the direct transmission of policy rates to term structure.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.88% as of t-1mo suggests elevated policy stance
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of 20-day forward windows when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and future economic contraction, causing investors to flee to safety by purchasing long-duration Treasuries, thereby depressing 10-year yields as prices rise and yields compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of triggered instances despite RSAFS decline, or forward SPX returns are positive in > 60% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% and payroll employment (PAYEMS) contracts month-over-month, labor market deterioration signals recession risk. This joint signal triggers flight-to-quality demand for Treasuries, compressing 10-year yields as safe-haven flows dominate.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4.5% AND PAYEMS declines > 50k persons in prior month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% and PAYEMS at 159626k as of t-3mo
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 102.0 (index 2017=100), signaling robust supply-side capacity and inflationary pressure. This triggers expectations of higher future policy rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market reprices terminal rate expectations upward.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly decline, indicating synchronized contraction in both residential and consumer demand. This dual-demand shock signals economic weakness and triggers a 10-year Treasury yield compression as investors reallocate to safe assets and growth expectations fall.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 1.5% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1307.0k and RSAFS at 733258.0M as of t-3mo and t-4mo
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or forward SPX returns are positive in > 65% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 325.0 (index 1982-1984=100) while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.5%, creating a negative real rate regime. This signals monetary accommodation relative to inflation, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as investors demand higher nominal yields to protect real returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 324.368 and FEDFUNDS at 3.88% as of t-3mo and t-1mo
regime_hint: stagflation or inflation persistence with policy lag
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50k persons added per month, signaling labor market softening. This always-on trend mechanism reflects weakening labor demand that precedes broader economic slowdown, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as recession probability increases and safe-haven demand rises.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 60% of 5-day forward windows when monthly PAYEMS growth < 50k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, indicating a regime shift toward economic stagnation. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts 10-year Treasury yields fall sharply as the market prices in lower terminal growth rates and increased recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23770.976B and UNRATE at 4.4% as of t-8mo and t-3mo
regime_hint: stagnation or early recession with labor market weakness
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or forward SPX returns are positive in > 70% of triggered 60-day windows.
